document.title: Message Channel
document.summary: A Message Channel is a virtual pipe that connects a sender to a receiver. The platform abstracts broker-specific channels behind IMessageChannel.
document.implementation: Ingestion broker abstraction (IKafkaProducer, INatsPublisher). Kafka topics and NATS JetStream subjects serve as message channels.

document.title: Message
document.summary: A Message is a discrete unit of data sent over a channel. The platform uses IntegrationEnvelope as the universal message wrapper.
document.implementation: IntegrationEnvelope<T> in Contracts — includes headers, correlation ID, business key, intent, timestamps, and typed payload.

document.title: Pipes and Filters
document.summary: Divide processing into independent steps (filters) connected by channels (pipes). Temporal activity chains implement this pattern.
document.implementation: Temporal workflow activities form filter chains. Processing.Transform provides ContentEnricher, ContentFilter, MessageNormalizer as composable filters.

document.title: Message Router
document.summary: A Message Router consumes a message from one channel and republishes it to a different channel depending on conditions.
document.implementation: Processing.Routing with ContentBasedRouter, DynamicRouter, RecipientListRouter, RoutingSlipRouter.

document.title: Message Translator
document.summary: Translates a message from one format to another so that systems with different data formats can communicate.
document.implementation: Processing.Translator with IMessageTranslator interface. Processing.Transform provides ContentEnricher and ContentFilter for field-level transformation.

document.title: Message Endpoint
document.summary: An application connects to a messaging channel using a Message Endpoint. The platform formalizes endpoints as consumers and gateways.
document.implementation: Ingestion layer: PollingConsumer, EventDrivenConsumer, SelectiveConsumer, DurableSubscriber. Gateway.Api provides HttpMessagingGateway.
